author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
Dryer hose off of the fan is a good idea; then fit some wet oasis foam into the end furthest the spray booth as a wet filter. 

Secondly; I noticed that you glued where the ends of the side boards bit up to the foot and head boards, and I suggest you take a quick second to explain why so you can transfer some of your experience along with your know how(Rob did a vid about something like that before and I am confident that he would agree).

Well done mate!

author: @indigogolf3051
text: I can't stand people shouting when a video starts I have sensitive hearing and it gives me a shock. Please don't do that.

author: @jamesjennings3098
text: A disposable respirator rated for spray paint might also be handy.

author: @docink6175
text: I like the foldability of it. I usually set up by a window and exhaust the chamber outside

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I should have run a pipe to extract the fumes outside, But I really like the idea of setting it up by a window. It makes it super easy. Thank you mate! :)

author: @chuxxsss
text: Luke run a water wall at the back, get rid of the spray.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hi mate, I'm unsure what a water wall is? But it sounds really cool! :)

replies.author: @chuxxsss
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Luke run a small plastic box at bottom and pump the water to the top of the wall then use some pcv pipe to spread it across your wall.

author: @ThunderDivine
text: This would do better as a photobooth all white gives amazing photos for say ebay etc.

author: @HunterValleyDan
text:
Hi Luke.  Good job mate.

Some 'constructive' criticism (pun intended).  You look a tad nervous.  I don't blame you, I'm too chicken to get in front of a camera, so well done.  But.  When you are showing things, slow down.  You are waving the board around like you're trying to film outside in a storm.

I'm too lazy to replay and slow-mo, so I couldn't see the edge of the board to see if its was solid or honeycomb.  You might not know what the product is called in another country, but if the viewers can see what it is, they can figure it out.  (I think it might be coreflute.)

Also, I'll repeat what others have said (XplaneZ).  A bigger fan, filter and preferably vent it outside if possible.

Good job though.  Keep 'em coming!

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: Extractor fan that blows everything straight into the workshop!  Get a hose to take the fumes outside  8-I

author: @bobweiram6321
text: That would also make a great Lightbox for taking photos.  I would hang some LED lights on the top so you can see better and a sheet of plastic that rolls over the front to keep out dust.

replies.text: That is an AWESOME idea! The plastic that rolls over the front I'm going to have to try! Cheers mate! :)

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: I've done something similar but with silver laminate flooring underlay, err good for reflecting light! Pringles tube using a piece of torn nbc material makes a great carbon filter but requires a stronger fan!;)

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: If you scaled this up and had a compressor spraying henna pigment you could make thousands turning Girls Orange!!!;) Japanese maple growers also taking note;)

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Very handy idea!!  Beats a cardboard box hands down - space in the shop is at a premium these days, anything that stows away is a winner in my book!!  You might consider a slot so you can insert a replaceable filter over the extractor fan.  😊   Maybe a strip of LED tape to light the interior of the booth from above, you can run it off the same power as the fan.  You can use plastic wrap or something as easily replaced to protect the strip so that you don't paint over the lights. 💡😊

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha I agree space is like gold dust! I really like the idea of a replaceable filter, which I might have to do. as for the led strip that is an addition that needs to happen! When the lighting gets a bit dark I tend to use my phone as a source of light when spraying. It works well enough but I only have 1 hand free. Thank you for the great suggestions! :)

author: @scotttovey
text:
You can improve the hanger by using a standard j-hook.

Drill a hole in the center of the top the diameter of a j-hook shaft and insert the j-hook from the inside.

Screw the j-hook into a bit of 2 x 4 or 2 x 2.

Now, when you want to turn your part around; simply turn the 2 x 2 or 2 x4 you screwed the j-hook into.

Hmm; if you don't want to drill a hole in the top, that plastic is thin enough that you could also use a hook with a magnet base and some angle iron to stick the magnet to.

Turning the angle iron turns the hook.

author: @defenestrator3090

Luke, what a neat idea.
